hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/5) binding by avoiding unfavorable electrostatic interactions. This combination will increase the selectivity margin (Δmargin) by differentiating SSTR2 from off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are structurally suboptimal and likely contain clashes or poor geometries that prevent them from passing QC. Tightening structural filters and focusing mutagenesis on non-pharmacophore positions will improve selectivity and yield.

proposed_changes.rationale: Tightening the clash filter will eliminate candidates with poor local geometry that may destabilize the binding interface or disrupt the pharmacophore.

Focusing design efforts on non-pharmacophore positions known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5) will help disrupt conserved pocket interactions while preserving the critical FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14 pharmacophore.
